key_insight: Agentic LLM systems can produce breast cancer treatment recommendations that are sometimes clinically relevant, but their reliability remains inconsistent across settings.
clinical_relevance: The results suggest these systems may assist decision support research, but they are not ready for unsupervised use in oncology care.
method: Evaluated 7 LLM pipelines, including baselines, tool-augmented systems, and multi-agent architectures.
Tested on 72 real breast cancer cases spanning stages I to IV.
Used 1,147 case-specific AIRG rubrics with real clinical decisions hidden from the evaluated models.
evidence_signal: The top configuration, Claude Opus 4.8 with D&C+SA, reached a global score of 0.594 ± 0.025, with performance varying by stage and clinical domain.
watch_item: Error patterns included wrong or missing recommendations, flawed justifications, citation mistakes, outdated claims, and overconfidence, raising safety concerns for real-world deployment.
paper_title: Agentic systems for breast cancer treatment recommendations
